behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: local module handoff (collectors -> aggregator -> summary input payload). The failure appears at module boundaries if one source is dropped/mis-keyed in orchestration. Integration is sufficient with in-process stubs for the three source clients and a spy/fake summary generator to assert payload completeness. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because behavior text only requires verifying the composed content passed onward, not real HTTP/network behavior; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no claim depends on model output quality or multi-agentic chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real orchestration modules with stubbed source adapters returning distinct sentinel fields for metrics/issues/deployments; execute one report run; assert the exact object/string sent to summary generation contains all three source sections and values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering. The key risk is interaction between source adapter failure handling and report assembly, not OS/network realism. Integration test with one adapter raising timeout/error and others succeeding can expose abort-vs-degrade bugs. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because concrete behavior text is about graceful fallback semantics, which can be reproduced with in-process failures; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness does not depend on model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run report pipeline with two source adapters returning normal data and one adapter raising a configured unavailability exception; verify run completes, output contains a `data unavailable` marker only for that section, and process does not abort.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: model output quality/judgment. The behavior is accuracy of narrative representation, which a deterministic stub would always satisfy and can hide real hallucination/omission errors. Minimum level is Agentic because the test must use the real summary model/service and evaluate whether key values are preserved. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified by behavior text 'accurately represents' (quality criterion on generated narrative), which cannot be validated against real failure modes with fixed mock outputs; Workflow is disqualified because this behavior evaluates a single generation step, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Feed controlled input datasets with known key figures into the real summary-generation dependency; score outputs against assertions/rubric for value preservation (numbers, trends, and critical statuses) across repeated runs to detect omissions or distortions.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: real external/system behavior (real APIs + real email delivery/inbox). Failure boundary includes end-to-end transport and inbox receipt, which mocks can hide (SMTP/API auth, provider acceptance, formatting/encoding issues, delivery path problems). System is minimum. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified by behavior text 'reaches ... inbox correctly,' which requires real delivery infrastructure; Agentic is disqualified because success criterion is delivery/completeness, not model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled report job in a test environment against reachable source endpoints and real email delivery path to a test mailbox; assert recipient inbox receives one message with expected subject/body sections and intact report content.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: process/run-boundary persistence plus external service outage behavior. Real failure often appears across separate scheduled executions (first run persists fallback artifact; later run re-attempts delivery), which in-process mocks can miss. System is minimum to validate file persistence and retry across actual job invocations with unreachable email service conditions. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified by behavior text 'next scheduled run,' implying cross-run behavior that must survive process boundaries; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because this is delivery resilience, not model-quality evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Run job once with email endpoint intentionally unreachable; verify fallback report file is written to disk. Trigger a subsequent scheduled execution in a fresh process with email restored; verify prior saved report is retried/sent and fallback state is cleared/updated correctly.
